name: Barley Mushroom Dressing
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add mushrooms and sprinkle with 1/8 teaspoon salt. Cook without stirring for 1 minute, then cook, stirring, for an additional 4 minutes or until the mushrooms are just softened. Transfer mushrooms to a large bowl.
2. Add 2 teaspoons olive oil to the skillet. Add onions, celery, carrots, and garlic. Sprinkle with another 1/8 teaspoon salt. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes. Add paprika, thyme, and sage. Cook for an additional minute. Transfer vegetables to the bowl with mushrooms.
3. Heat remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add barley and cook, stirring, for 1 minute. Pour in chicken broth and bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 50 minutes or until barley is tender but not mushy.
4. Preheat the oven to 350°F. Lightly coat a deep 2-1/2-quart casserole dish with nonstick cooking spray.
5. Stir the barley mixture, parsley, and orange juice into the bowl with vegetables. Add 3/4 teaspoon salt and 1/4 teaspoon pepper. If the mixture seems dry, stir in a little water or chicken broth. Transfer to the prepared casserole dish and cover with aluminum foil.
6. Bake for 30 to 40 minutes, uncovering the dressing for the last 15 minutes. Makes 12 servings.
Approximate nutritional analysis: 119 calories per serving; 4g protein; 20g carbohydrate; 3g fat (22% of calories); 4g fiber; 346mg sodium; 35% Daily Value for vitamin A.
American Health -- November 1995
